title: Tapping to release money procrastination
subtitle: A tapping practice for when financial tasks keep moving down the list.
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 45 of the MONEY program. The script works with the weight that attaches to money tasks: calls, forms, follow-ups — and makes them easy to defer.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use before sitting down to a money task you've been avoiding — a call to make, a form to fill, a number to look at.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 45 of the MONEY program. The script targets the particular drag that attaches to money admin: calls, forms, follow-ups, financial conversations — and makes them feel heavier than they are.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I sometimes delay important money tasks, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I put off calls, forms, or follow-ups, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I feel heavy starting big projects, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round (repeat x3)
**Eyebrow:** I avoid financial admin.
**Side of eye:** I think it will be too hard or boring.
**Under eye:** I tell myself I'll do it later.
**Under nose:** Later often turns into weeks.
**Chin:** I feel frustrated by my own delays.
**Collarbone:** I know action creates growth.
**Under arm:** I'm ready to break this habit.
**Top of head:** I free myself from money procrastination.
Reframe to positive (repeat x3)
**Eyebrow:** I handle money tasks quickly and easily.
**Side of eye:** I enjoy getting them done.
**Under eye:** I feel lighter once I act.
**Under nose:** I choose progress over delay.
**Chin:** I trust small steps to build big wealth.
**Collarbone:** I make action my default setting.
**Under arm:** I love the clarity that comes from moving forward.
**Top of head:** I act promptly and watch abundance grow.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 45 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). EFT tapping developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
